name: As Pontes - Xermade Loop
name_original: As Pontes - vuelta por Alto de Sotavento
description: Cycle 30.0 miles (48.3 km) through Galicia's Parque Natural das Fragas do Eume, climbing Alto de Sotavento for scenic views.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around As Pontes de García Rodríguez traverse a landscape shaped by both natural beauty and industrial heritage. The region features the expansive artificial Lake As Pontes, Spain's largest, offering routes along its perimeter. Cyclists can also explore the dense Atlantic Forest of Fragas do Eume Natural Park, characterized by winding roads and river valleys. The diverse terrain includes both flatter sections and more challenging climbs, reflecting the area's suitability for varied cycling experiences. Best road cycling routes around As Pontes De García Rodríguez The most popular road cycling route is As Pontes - Xermade Loop, a 30.0 miles (48.3 km) trail that takes 2 hours 20 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers a balanced ride through the local countryside. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Ribeira Reservoir – Lago de As Pontes loop from A Ribeira Nova, a moderate 32.7 miles (52.6 km) path. This route provides scenic views around the reservoir and Lake As Pontes. Local road cyclists also love the View of A Lameira at sunset – Lago de As Pontes loop from As Pontes de García Rodríguez, a 58.4 miles (94.0 km) trail leading through the area surrounding Lake As Pontes, often completed in about 4 hours 58 minutes. Road cycling around As Pontes de García Rodríguez is defined by the artificial Lake As Pontes, the Atlantic Forest of Fragas do Eume Natural Park, and diverse terrain. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes. The routes in As Pontes de García Rodríguez are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.9 stars from over 5 reviews. More than 400 road cyclists have used komoot to explore As Pontes de García Rodríguez's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available in As Pontes de García Rodríguez?
A: There are over 45 road cycling routes around As Pontes de García Rodríguez listed on komoot. These routes cater to various skill levels, with options ranging from easy to difficult.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when road cycling around As Pontes de García Rodríguez?
A: The region offers diverse terrain, from flatter sections around the expansive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5325663" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ribeira Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; and Lake As Pontes to more challenging climbs within the dense Atlantic Forest of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7546440" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fragas do Eume Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;. The area has even hosted professional cycling events, indicating its suitability for varied gradients.

Q: Are there any easy road cycling routes suitable for beginners in As Pontes de García Rodríguez?
A: Yes, the region offers several easy road cycling routes. While specific easy routes are not highlighted in the top selections, the overall network includes options for less experienced cyclists, particularly around the perimeter of Lake As Pontes where the terrain tends to be flatter.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks I can see along road cycling routes?
A: Road cycling routes often pass by significant natural features and landmarks. You can explore the unique artificial Lake As Pontes, Spain's largest, or cycle through the ancient Atlantic Forest of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7546440" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fragas do Eume Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;. Within the park, you might encounter the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/936577" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monastery of Saint John of Caaveiro&lt;/a&gt; or the picturesque &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5711118" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Cristina Bridge&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any routes that feature waterfalls?
A: Yes, the area around As Pontes de García Rodríguez is home to several beautiful waterfalls. Cyclists can find routes that pass near &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3566939" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fervenza de Naraío&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6271785" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fervenza do Río Xestosa&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7476337" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fervenza de Bermui&lt;/a&gt;, offering scenic stops during your ride.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes around As Pontes de García Rodríguez?
A: Many of the routes in the region are designed as loops, allowing for circular journeys. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20160057" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ribeira Reservoir – Lago de As Pontes loop from A Ribeira Nova&lt;/a&gt; offers scenic views around the reservoir and Lake As Pontes. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31884155" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lago de As Pontes loop from A Casilla&lt;/a&gt;, which provides a moderate ride around the lake.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in As Pontes de García Rodríguez?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.9 stars from over 5 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse terrain, the stunning views around Lake As Pontes, and the natural beauty of Fragas do Eume Natural Park.

Q: Is there parking available near the road cycling routes?
A: While specific parking locations are not detailed for every route, As Pontes de García Rodríguez is a municipality that supports outdoor activities. You can typically find parking in the town center or at designated access points for natural areas like Lake As Pontes or Fragas do Eume Natural Park, which serve as good starting points for many routes.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in As Pontes de García Rodríguez?
A: The region generally experiences a mild climate. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and lush landscapes, making them ideal for road cycling. Summer can also be enjoyable, especially for routes near the lake, though it might be warmer. Winter cycling is possible but may involve cooler temperatures and more unpredictable weather.

Q: Are there any longer distance road cycling routes for experienced cyclists?
A: Yes, for experienced cyclists seeking longer distances and more elevation, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20161958" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of A Lameira at sunset – Lago de As Pontes loop from As Pontes de García Rodríguez&lt;/a&gt; cover approximately 94 kilometers and include significant elevation gain, often taking around 5 hours to complete.

Q: Can I combine road cycling with exploring the industrial heritage of As Pontes de García Rodríguez?
A: Yes, the region has a 'Ruta de la Experiencia Industrial' which allows visitors to explore its industrial landscape. While primarily designed for walking or general cycling, road cyclists can incorporate elements of this heritage into their rides, observing how the area has transformed from its industrial past into a hub for outdoor recreation, particularly around the rehabilitated Lake As Pontes.

Q: Are there any family-friendly road cycling options?
A: While some routes involve challenging terrain, the flatter sections around Lake As Pontes are generally more suitable for families. These areas offer scenic, less strenuous rides, allowing for a more relaxed experience for all ages.
tips: The town is famous for the lignite mine and the concrete chimneys of its thermal power plant on the shores of Lake As Pontes

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route primarily consists of firm, paved tracks, making it ideal for road bikes. While categorized as moderate, expect a steady effort with around 620 meters of climbing, requiring good physical condition.

faqs.question: Are there any notable landmarks or scenic viewpoints along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route features the artificial Lago de As Pontes and includes the climb to Alto de Sotavento. The area is known for its diverse Galician landscapes, including dense forests, lush meadows, and green hillsides, offering scenic views.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: Galicia's climate suggests that spring and early autumn would be ideal, offering pleasant temperatures and less chance of extreme weather. These seasons typically provide the best conditions for enjoying the region's natural beauty.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Given its moderate difficulty and approximately 620 meters of elevation gain over 48.3 kilometers, this route is best suited for cyclists with a good fitness level. Beginners might find the climbing challenging.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead for this route?
faqs.answer: While specific parking details for the exact start point are not provided, As Pontes de García Rodríguez is the primary town for access. Look for public parking areas within the town, which should provide convenient access to the route's beginning.

faqs.question: Does the route pass through any other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with other paths. You'll find it intersects with parts of the Enlace do Camiño do Norte co Camiño Inglés, Enlace gravel, Senda da Ribeira, Camiño Real, Rúa Rego Bellón, Camiño da Balsa, Paseo da Constitución, Ponte Peonil, and O Camiño da Fraga do Aro.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This is a road cycling route primarily on paved surfaces. While there are no specific restrictions against dogs on leashes on public roads, always be mindful of traffic and local regulations, especially in populated areas or near natural parks. It's generally not designed as a dog-friendly hiking trail.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay entrance fees for this route or the surrounding area?
faqs.answer: No specific permits or entrance fees are required for cycling this route. The area around As Pontes and Alto de Sotavento is generally accessible to the public. If you venture into the nearby Parque Natural das Fragas do Eume, be aware of any specific regulations they might have, though this route primarily uses public roads.

faqs.question: Are there cafes, pubs, or accommodation options nearby for cyclists?
faqs.answer: As Pontes de García Rodríguez, the town near the start of the route, offers various amenities including cafes, pubs, and accommodation options. It's a good place to refuel or stay before or after your ride.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a ride on this route?
faqs.answer: Given the route's length and elevation, bring plenty of water, snacks, a repair kit for your racebike, and appropriate cycling attire. A helmet is essential, and consider layers as weather can change, especially at higher elevations like Alto de Sotavento.

faqs.question: Is there public transport available to reach As Pontes with a bicycle?
faqs.answer: Public transport options in Galicia, such as regional buses or trains, may allow bicycles, often with specific conditions or additional fees. It's advisable to check with the local transport providers (e.g., Monbus, Renfe) in advance for their current bicycle policies and availability to As Pontes de García Rodríguez.
